patterns_discovered.description: Project uses conventional static files directory structure with separate subdirectories for different asset types
Uses .gitkeep files to ensure empty directories are tracked in version control
Project structure indicates a web application with static assets served separately from application code
patterns_discovered.implementation: Dedicated static/ directory at project root level

gotchas_discovered.description: Git does not track empty directories by default; .gitkeep files are a workaround convention
gotchas_discovered.impact: Developers must remember to commit .gitkeep files for project structure to be preserved on clone

gotchas_discovered.description: Flat structure for static assets may require future organization as project grows (e.g., subdirectories within css/ for components)
gotchas_discovered.impact: May need refactoring if static assets library becomes large

approach_outcome.implementation_method: Direct directory structure creation with .gitkeep markers
approach_outcome.notes: Straightforward infrastructure task completed successfully on first attempt

recommendations.description: Add documentation explaining the static directory structure and asset organization conventions for team members
Consider implementing CSS/JS minification and optimization processes as assets are added
Create starter templates or example files in each directory to guide developers on asset organization
Establish guidelines for whether large binary assets (images) should be stored in Git or a separate CDN/storage system
